proposed_hypotheses.text: A 10-year higher `age_years` value is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical stage, performance status, biomarkers, tumor burden, inflammatory markers, and treatment indicators.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS: pfs_months ~ age_years/10 + sex_female + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + node_positive + postmenopausal + ER/PR/HER2/BRCA/PIK3CA markers + ki67_pct/10 + tumor_size_cm + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l/100 + weight_loss_pct_6mo/5 + crp_mg_l/10 + nlr + treatment indicators.
analyses.result_summary: In the adjusted model, each 10-year higher age_years was associated with 1.7569 higher pfs_months (SE 0.0051; z=343.57; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `ecog_ps` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `ecog_ps` set to 0 after adjustment for disease features, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for ecog_ps.
analyses.result_summary: `ecog_ps`=1 was associated with 1.1717 fewer pfs_months than `ecog_ps`=0 (SE 0.0075; z=-156.03; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `stage_iv` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients with `stage_iv` set to 0 after adjustment for other prognostic features and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for stage_iv.
analyses.result_summary: `stage_iv`=1 was associated with 1.5489 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0111; z=-139.39; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `has_brain_mets` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without brain metastases, independent of stage, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for has_brain_mets.
analyses.result_summary: `has_brain_mets`=1 was associated with 0.9833 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0168; z=-58.43; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `node_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than node-negative patients after adjustment for stage, metastatic disease, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for node_positive.
analyses.result_summary: `node_positive`=1 had a small adjusted association of -0.0177 pfs_months (SE 0.0102; z=-1.74; p=0.0821), not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `er_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than ER-negative patients after adjustment for other receptor markers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for er_positive.
analyses.result_summary: `er_positive`=1 was associated with 0.5313 higher pfs_months (SE 0.0149; z=35.63; p=4.67e-278).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for `er_positive`, patients with `pr_positive` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than PR-negative patients.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for pr_positive while er_positive is included.
analyses.result_summary: After ER adjustment, `pr_positive`=1 had an adjusted association of only 0.0143 pfs_months (SE 0.0143; z=1.01; p=0.315), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than HER2-negative patients after adjustment for hormone receptors, other biomarkers, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: `her2_positive`=1 was associated with 0.4708 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0142; z=-33.22; p=4.71e-242).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `her2_low` set to 1 have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients without HER2-low status after adjustment for `her2_positive`, hormone receptors, disease severity, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for her2_low while her2_positive is included.
analyses.result_summary: `her2_low`=1 had essentially no adjusted association with pfs_months: 0.0019 months (SE 0.0113; z=0.17; p=0.865).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 have lower mean `pfs_months` than patients without `pik3ca_mutation` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for pik3ca_mutation.
analyses.result_summary: `pik3ca_mutation`=1 was associated with 0.5816 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0107; z=-54.41; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for clinical severity, receptor markers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using ki67_pct/10.
analyses.result_summary: Each 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct` was associated with 0.1889 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0042; z=-45.14; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` is associated with higher mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, inflammatory markers, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using albumin_g_dl.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1 g/dL higher `albumin_g_dl` was associated with 0.4756 higher pfs_months (SE 0.0102; z=46.86; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using weight_loss_pct_6mo/5.
analyses.result_summary: Each 5 percentage-point higher `weight_loss_pct_6mo` was associated with 0.3840 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0066; z=-58.21; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for disease severity, biomarkers, other labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using ldh_u_l/100.
analyses.result_summary: Each 100 U/L higher `ldh_u_l` was associated with 0.0518 fewer pfs_months (SE 0.0063; z=-8.22; p=2.06e-16).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After accounting for albumin, LDH, weight loss, stage, biomarkers, and treatments, higher `crp_mg_l` and higher `nlr` remain independently associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using crp_mg_l/10.
analyses.result_summary: Each 10 mg/L higher `crp_mg_l` had an adjusted association of -0.0011 pfs_months (SE 0.0054; z=-0.20; p=0.842), not significant.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using nlr.
analyses.result_summary: Each 1-unit higher `nlr` had an adjusted association of -0.0015 pfs_months (SE 0.0024; z=-0.61; p=0.544), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher `tumor_size_cm` is associated with lower mean `pfs_months` after adjustment for stage, nodal status, biomarkers, labs, and treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using tumor_size_cm.
analyses.result_summary: `tumor_size_cm` had no meaningful adjusted association with pfs_months: 0.0013 months per cm (SE 0.0029; z=0.44; p=0.661).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving `treatment_palbociclib` have higher mean `pfs_months` than patients not receiving `treatment_palbociclib` after adjustment for clinical severity, biomarkers, labs, and other treatments.

analyses.code: Same adjusted OLS as h1, using the coefficient for treatment_palbociclib.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_palbociclib` was associated with 1.1014 higher pfs_months (SE 0.0107; z=103.13; p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1 than in ER-negative patients.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib, er_positive, their interaction, and the base covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_palbociclib` x `er_positive` interaction was +1.5593 pfs_months (SE 0.0222; z=70.21; p<1e-300). Adjusted subgroup models estimated no palbociclib benefit in ER-negative patients (-0.0029 months, p=0.366) and a large benefit in ER-positive patients (+1.5704 months, p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in tumors with high proliferation, measured as a 10 percentage-point higher `ki67_pct`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib, ki67_pct/10, their interaction, and the base covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_palbociclib` x (`ki67_pct`/10) interaction was -0.5316 pfs_months per 10 percentage points (SE 0.0084; z=-63.14; p<1e-300). Using the top quartile cutoff (`ki67_pct` >= 19.6), the high-Ki67 interaction was -1.4613 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive association of `treatment_palbociclib` with `pfs_months` is smaller in patients with `pik3ca_mutation` set to 1 than in patients without `pik3ca_mutation`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_palbociclib, pik3ca_mutation, their interaction, and the base covariates; a joint model also included palbociclib interactions with er_positive and ki67_pct/10.
analyses.result_summary: The pairwise `treatment_palbociclib` x `pik3ca_mutation` interaction was -1.6655 pfs_months (SE 0.0212; z=-78.67; p<1e-300). In the joint modifier model with ER and Ki-67 interactions included, the interaction remained -1.6494 months (p<1e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `treatment_tamoxifen` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `er_positive` set to 1.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS base model for treatment_tamoxifen main effect.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main association for `treatment_tamoxifen` was -0.0077 pfs_months (SE 0.0111; z=-0.70; p=0.487), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_tamoxifen, er_positive, their interaction, and the base covariates.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_tamoxifen` x `er_positive` interaction was +0.0108 pfs_months (SE 0.0267; z=0.41; p=0.685), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `treatment_trastuzumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with `her2_positive` set to 1 or `her2_amplification` set to 1.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS base model for treatment_trastuzumab main effect.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main association for `treatment_trastuzumab` was -0.0095 pfs_months (SE 0.0127; z=-0.75; p=0.454), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab x her2_positive.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_trastuzumab` x `her2_positive` interaction was +0.0108 pfs_months (SE 0.0359; z=0.30; p=0.764), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with treatment_trastuzumab x her2_amplification.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_trastuzumab` x `her2_amplification` interaction was -0.0756 pfs_months (SE 0.0822; z=-0.92; p=0.358), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `treatment_olaparib` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in patients with either `brca1_mutation` or `brca2_mutation` set to 1.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS base model for treatment_olaparib main effect.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted main association for `treatment_olaparib` was -0.0264 pfs_months (SE 0.0170; z=-1.55; p=0.121), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS with any_brca=(brca1_mutation OR brca2_mutation), treatment_olaparib, and their interaction; brca1_mutation and brca2_mutation were omitted from covariates to avoid collinearity with any_brca.
analyses.result_summary: The `treatment_olaparib` x any-BRCA interaction was +0.0438 pfs_months (SE 0.0874; z=0.50; p=0.616), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The association of `treatment_pembrolizumab` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).
The association of `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` with `pfs_months` is positive overall and is larger in triple-negative patients (`er_positive`=0, `pr_positive`=0, and `her2_positive`=0).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS base model for treatment_pembrolizumab main effect plus an interaction model with triple_negative.
analyses.result_summary: The base adjusted main association for `treatment_pembrolizumab` was -0.0195 pfs_months (p=0.170). In the triple-negative interaction model the main effect was -0.0414 months (p=0.020), while the `treatment_pembrolizumab` x triple-negative interaction was +0.0665 months (SE 0.0391; p=0.0886), not significant.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS base model for treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan main effect plus an interaction model with triple_negative.
analyses.result_summary: `treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan` had a small nominal adjusted main association of +0.0335 pfs_months (p=0.0487), but the triple-negative interaction was -0.0217 months (SE 0.0461; p=0.638), not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for stage, brain metastases, biomarkers, labs, and treatments, additional metastatic-site indicators such as `visceral_mets` and common comorbidity indicators such as `diabetes_mellitus` and `hypertension` remain independently associated with lower mean `pfs_months`.

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS adding derived visceral_mets=(liver_mets OR contralateral_lung_mets OR adrenal_mets) to the base covariates.
analyses.result_summary: Derived `visceral_mets` had essentially no adjusted association with pfs_months beyond the core severity variables: -0.0011 months (SE 0.0119; z=-0.09; p=0.926).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS adding diabetes_mellitus to the base covariates and treatment indicators.
analyses.result_summary: `diabetes_mellitus` had no independent adjusted association: +0.0021 pfs_months (SE 0.0123; z=0.18; p=0.861).

analyses.code: Adjusted OLS adding hypertension to the base covariates and treatment indicators.
analyses.result_summary: `hypertension` had no independent adjusted association: -0.0002 pfs_months (SE 0.0102; z=-0.02; p=0.986).
